operation: Start with an XY workplane tagged as 'base', create a 101mm x 22mm x 62mm box that is centered on the X and Z axes, not centered on the Y axis. Return to the 'base' tagged workplane, shift the origin to (0, 22) and tag this workplane 'lower', then create a 98mm x 40mm x 62mm box that is centered on the X and Z axes, attached to the front face of the first box. Return to the 'lower' tagged workplane, shift the origin to (0, 40), draw a half profile consisting of a 1mm negative Y vertical line, a 32mm positive X horizontal line, a 1mm positive Y vertical line, a line to point (15,15), and a horizontal line back to X=0. Mirror this half profile across the Y axis to form a closed symmetric profile, then extrude the profile 31mm in both positive and negative Z directions to form a 62mm thick solid attached to the front of the middle box.
geometry: This is a prismatic solid with uniform thickness along the Z axis, fully symmetric about its central X-Z plane. It is made of three consecutive connected segments arranged along the Y axis: a wider rectangular rear segment, a slightly narrower rectangular middle segment attached to the front of the rear segment, and a front segment that tapers inward from the middle segment's width to a pointed forward edge with angled side faces.
function: This is a protective cover for GT series timing belt drive assemblies, designed to enclose moving drive components to prevent accidental contact and contain debris. Its stepped and tapered profile is engineered to fit closely around standard GT drive components of varying sizes.
